 - New episodes of Garlic Jr saga every Saturday at 10 am.
 - Season 4 will air in September; first six episodes to be released May 16 on tape.
 - Burger King to run DBZ promotion May 29th to June 18th, will offer action figures and exclusive videotapes.
 - Sub-titled DBZ on DVD format coming in May/June.
 - DBZ Game for the PSX, PC, Mac, and possibly a Nintendo system due out Q1 2001.
 - Movie #4 coming subbed/dubbed sometime this year.

*We are NOT accepting hosting submissions*



Would you sign up for a DBZ newsletter if we offered one (it would recap recent stories in our news)?

Most definitely
Probably
No

Statistics

View News In:
French | German | Italian
Portugese | Spanish


Monday - 04/17/2000

SkullMac @ 16:59 pst - Server move complete
After only a minimal downtime, we are now located on a new set of servers, which should fix out poll problems and provide overall faster access.

SkullMac @ 15:47 pst - Server move in progress.
Our site may act sporadically for a while. As we noted last week, GameFAN is switching our site to a faster array of servers. While this is occurring, you may be unable to access the site. GameFAN's admin has informed me that any problems will resolve themselves "as soon as the files are uncompressed," which I'm guessing shouldn't take to long.

SkullMac @ 10:23 pst - An explanation of episode number differences
Several of you have asked why there is a difference in the English and Japanese episode numbers in the list below. Quite simply, it is due to the large number of cuts in season's one and two (and season three to some extent). Some episodes were hacked down to less then 20% of their original content, while others were cut out in their entirety.

Sunday - 04/16/2000

Mr.E @ 20:18 pst - The Dollhouse...
Day late on this one...but Meri has released yet another feature film involving a news cast and other fun stuff. It's quite zany, and funnier than some of her other films. Have a look!

Mr.E @ 16:53 pst - There is no God...
A few weeks ago Greg Werner of The Ultimate DBZ Info Site played a little April Fool's joke and announced that a live-action DBZ movie was going to be released here in the states. Well, it turns out this silly prank is not far from the truth. Get a load of this. Alright, so the movie was completed several years ago, the acting is poor, and there are no "matrix-style" effects. But that still doesn't mean it can't be a gre...wait no, it's cheesy trash that will make any DB fan cringe in pain. And we get it on DVD. In English. Joy. ^_- (Thanks to corey for sending this in)

SkullMac @ 15:36 pst - TV Schedule Updated
As I promiced I would do last week, but was unable to do so, I have updated the TV Schedule to reflect Cartoon Network's new "Rising Sun" anime block. In addition to that, I have appended some new times to the Polish schedule, and made the layout easier to read on PCs to boot.

SkullMac @ 15:15 pst - Up-to-date Release List
Thanks to the help of Piccolenks, I have organized a short list of release dates for the next seven tapes.

Saga Tape Name English Numbers Japanese Numbers Release Date
Trunks Mysterious Youth 103-105 118-120 May 23
Trunks Prelude to Terror 106-108 121-123 May 23
Trunks "Goku Driving Episodes" [Burger King Promotion] 109-110 124-125 May 29
Androids Invasion 111-113 126-128 July 25th
Androids Dr. Gero 114-116 129-131 July 25th
Androids Assassins 117-119 132-134 September 26th
Androids Invincible 120-122 135-137 September 26th

Saturday - 04/15/2000

SkullMac @ 07:58 pst - Movie 9 Review and Sumary Up.
With much thanks to John Morey, Message Board moderator and author of the Fanfic Dark Ki, we have posted a review and summary of Movie 9: The Galaxy's in Danger! The Super Awesome Guy! In any event, I have also temporarily removed the poll from the Movie Guide page, as it was acting very sporadic and making it difficult to read the reviews.

Thursday - 04/13/2000

Mr.E @ 21:07 pst - FTP Problems...
Yeah, I know...Gamefan's FTP box is completely shot, so the movie files will be gone temporarily while they set up a new machine (which will take a week or two). Sorry for the inconvenience...I should have another movie review ready by tomorrow to tide you guys over...

Wednesday - 04/12/2000

Mr.E @ 22:29 pst - Figures.com Contest...
Here's your opportunity to win some DBZ stuff...figures.com is holding a contest to win a giant Goku figure, and a number of other goodies (But it's Irwin...hehe). There's no obligation, so you might as well enter...what have you to lose?

Mr.E @ 20:37 pst - YTV News...
Looks like Gundam W will be getting some airtime on our favourite Canadian station (blame Canada...that should have won the oscar...):

Hi there! We have great news for you!!! Due to popular demand, YTV has acquired the first three (3) episodes of the animated series, 'Gundam Wing!'

Since you have asked us previously about anime, we decided to drop you a line and let you know this exciting news before anyone else. Mark your calendar! YTV will be airing these three episodes back-to-back on Monday, April 24, 200 from 5:30 to 7pm E/P. After you have seen the first three episodes on YTV, you can take part in an interactive poll on our groovy website at www.ytv.com, where you can give us your opinion on this new show. From there, YTV will then decide, based on viewer response, if more episodes will find a regular place in our upcoming schedule. There will also be a special 'Gundam Wing' section on our website starting Monday, April 17. Check it out!

We hope that you are happy to hear this anime news. Thanks for taking the time to write to us, and we hope you will enjoy, 'Gundam Wing.' Thanks for watching and remember to Keep it Weird!!!

Your Friends at YTV

I know it's not DB related, but Canadian Dragonball fans may find it interesting ;P Our regards to Chris Serio for forwarding this to us...

Mr.E @ 19:44 pst - Server Issues...
We'll finally be moving to the load-sharing server system on Monday...I had said that we were doing this before, but other things came up, and it had to be postponed. This should fix our constantly resetting main poll, which will be on vacation until the move is done. The FTP server has been intermittent as of late...sometimes it's up, sometimes it's down. If by chance the main site does go down for an hour or so, you can always go to the board to find out what's happening...

Mr.E @ 19:33 pst - More Burger King Info...
We now have an end date for the promotion. This is from a magazine entitled the "Nations Restaurant News:"

Burger King to launch "Dragon Ball Z" promotion

Miami-- Burger King Corp., whose Pokemon promotion was so successful that it ran twice, plans to tie in to another popular Japanese import, "Dragon Ball Z" with a big kids Meal promotion May 29- June 18. "Dragon Ball Z" is an animated series airing on the Cartonn Network. Seven toy figurines based on the program will be included in meals. Exclusive trading cards also will be available. National TV adverising will support the promotion.

The show has been popular in Japan for a number of years and has attracted millions of kids to the Cartoon Network. The series "follows the story of Goku, who was sent by his people, the Saiyans, to destroy Earth," acording to the network.

The national TV exposure will be a HUGE boost to DBZ...currently, there is no advertising for the series beyond the stations it airs on. BK will have to be careful however not to dumb the promotions down to Pokemon's level, we are trying to attract an older crowd, not a younger one... ^_^ Thanks to Adel Hodiwala for typing this up for us...

SkullMac @ 17:44 pst - Game section update and more card info
In an effort to give it a fresh look, the Games section has received a minor facelift. Of a bit more interest, those of you who have been looking for walk throughs of the SNES RPGs are in luck. New FAQs have been posted for both Super Gokuden 1 and 2.

On the card front, Garret Fitzgerald has informed us that the retail prices for the Starter Decks will be around $11 and will include 54 cards. Each booster pack will cost close to $3.50 and contain 9 cards.



Previous News


Disclaimers
Planet Namek is just a fansite, we did NOT create Dragonball, nor did we have any part in the production process.

Dragonball, DragonballZ, and DragonballGT were produced by Toei Entertainment, Bird Studios, and Akira Toriyama.
This site is not meant to infinge on any copyrights, it is simply here to encourage people to watch the Dragonball series.

Although we did not create the series, we did take a lot of time to work on this site and bring you original files and information. Please honor our efforts by not stealing our files/layout from us. Thank you.

Web Page Design © Planet Namek


Best viewed in 800 by 600 resolution or better & thousands of colors or more.
For the best experience, set your browser to view images "better" rather than "faster."
